name: Alabama
esa_admin: Alabama Department of Revenue; ClassWallet contracted for program support/distribution
esa_funding_amount: $7,000 participating-school student; $2,000 home education student, capped at $4,000 per family
esa_eligible_grades: K5-12 Alabama residents; universal by 2027-28, income/priority phase-in earlier
esa_application_deadline: 2026-27 portal closed March 31, 2026
esa_max_reimbursement: Program account amount; home education cap $2,000/student, $4,000/family
esa_notes: State tax-credit ESA administered by ALDOR. Private school and home education have different award levels.

name: Arkansas
esa_admin: Arkansas Department of Education / Office of School Choice and Parent Empowerment
esa_funding_amount: Up to 90% of prior-year state foundation funding; about $6,800-$7,000 range depending year
esa_eligible_grades: K-12 Arkansas students meeting program criteria; by 2025-26 broadly/universally eligible
esa_application_deadline: 2026-27 application/renewal period: March 9-June 1, 2026
esa_reimbursement_url: ClassWallet portal via Arkansas EFA family page
esa_notes: Families apply/renew annually for each student. Reimbursements submitted through ClassWallet.

name: Arizona
esa_admin: Arizona Department of Education
esa_funding_amount: Varies by student/grade/weights; funded quarterly; approximate amount chart maintained by ADE
esa_eligible_grades: K-12 universal eligibility
esa_application_deadline: Rolling / applications accepted for 2026-2027; ADE says processing within 30 days
esa_notes: ClassWallet is the financial management platform. ESA can support education at home but families sign ESA contract and spending rules apply.

name: Florida
esa_portal_name: Family Empowerment Scholarship / Personalized Education Program
esa_admin: Florida Department of Education oversight; Scholarship Funding Organizations including Step Up For Students and AAA Scholarship Foundation
esa_funding_amount: Average private school scholarship about $8,000; PEP/FES-UA values vary by grade, county and disability matrix
esa_eligible_grades: Florida residents eligible for K-12 public school; FES-UA includes eligible students with unique abilities, including some pre-K ages
esa_uses_other_platform: Step Up For Students EMA / MyScholarShop; AAA Scholarship systems
esa_application_deadline: Open until program caps/funding limits; PEP 2026-27 cap noted at 140,000 students
esa_max_reimbursement: Varies by scholarship branch and award amount
esa_notes: Florida has multiple K-12 scholarship paths. PEP is the ESA-style option for students not enrolled full-time in public/private school; FES-UA is ESA-style for students with qualifying disabilities.

name: Georgia
esa_admin: Georgia Education Savings Authority / Georgia Student Finance Commission
esa_funding_amount: Up to $6,500 per eligible student, paid quarterly
esa_eligible_grades: K-12 students meeting low-performing attendance-zone and other statutory criteria; includes private school and home study use cases
esa_application_deadline: 2026-27 remaining windows: August 1-31 and November 1-30
esa_reimbursement_url: Odyssey platform via Georgia Promise portal
esa_notes: Eligibility is not universal; student must be zoned for an eligible/low-performing public school and meet program rules.

name: Iowa
program_status: active; universal K-12 for accredited nonpublic enrollment
esa_admin: Iowa Department of Education; Odyssey handles applications/payments
esa_funding_amount: Anticipated $8,148 per eligible student for 2026-27
esa_eligible_grades: K-12 Iowa resident students attending accredited nonpublic schools
esa_application_deadline: 2026-27 application period April 16-June 30, 2026
esa_notes: Not a pure independent homeschool reimbursement program; funds are tied to accredited nonpublic school enrollment and qualified expenses.

name: Indiana
esa_admin: Indiana Treasurer of State
esa_funding_amount: Varies; up to $20,000 for students with disabilities and up to $8,000 for eligible siblings
esa_eligible_grades: K-12 students with disabilities and, since 2024, eligible siblings; income cap applies
esa_application_deadline: Program cycle opens annually; verify current priority window on INESA portal
esa_notes: Cannot combine with Indiana Choice Scholarship voucher; ClassWallet partnership expanded for ESA management.

name: Kentucky
program_status: not active; prior EOA struck down
esa_admin: Kentucky Department of Revenue page exists for tax credit history; no active K-12 ESA portal
esa_notes: Kentucky Supreme Court held the EOA Act unconstitutional in 2022; treat as litigation/dead program, not active ESA.

name: Louisiana
esa_admin: Louisiana Department of Education; Odyssey platform support
esa_funding_amount: 2025-26 tiers cited by Odyssey: $5,243 / $7,626 / up to $15,253 depending income/disability; 2026-27 appropriation pending at search time
esa_eligible_grades: K-12 Louisiana students; universal phased/priority rules apply
esa_application_deadline: 2026-27 application window opened March 1 and closed March 16, 2026
esa_reimbursement_url: Odyssey portal / LA GATOR family support
esa_notes: State noted April-June 2026 legislature would decide 2026-27 funding; award notifications estimated June/July 2026 via Odyssey.

name: Missouri
program_status: active tax-credit ESA; not state-funded universal ESA
esa_admin: Missouri State Treasurer; scholarships issued through approved Educational Assistance Organizations
esa_funding_amount: Awards may reach up to 175% of state adequacy target depending need and EAO funding
esa_eligible_grades: K-12 eligible Missouri students under program priority/eligibility rules
esa_application_deadline: 2026-27 first application deadline reported April 3, 2026; summer window also reported
esa_max_reimbursement: Depends on EAO/funding and statutory caps
esa_notes: Tax-credit ESA funded through donations to EAOs, not the same operating model as direct state ESA wallets.

name: Mississippi
esa_admin: Mississippi Department of Education
esa_funding_amount: Varies annually; 2024-25 maximum reimbursement source cited $7,829
esa_eligible_grades: Students with special needs meeting ESA criteria
esa_application_deadline: Verify annual MDE application window
esa_max_reimbursement: Annual ESA maximum set by formula/appropriation
esa_notes: Special-needs-only ESA; 2024 PEER review found 90% of funds disbursed in FY2023-FY2024.

name: Montana
program_status: established but litigation-blocked as of Dec 2025 reporting; verify before productizing
esa_portal_name: Special Needs Equal Opportunity Education Savings Account
esa_admin: Montana Office of Public Instruction
esa_funding_amount: 95% of eligible calculated amount deposited for allowable education resources under statute; exact award varies
esa_eligible_grades: Students with disabilities under IDEA meeting additional criteria
esa_application_deadline: Blocked/uncertain; verify current court status and OPI notice
esa_notes: Watchlist: Montana Free Press reported a district judge blocked HB393 program in December 2025.

name: North Carolina
esa_admin: North Carolina State Education Assistance Authority
esa_funding_amount: Base $9,000 annual award; $17,000 for certain designated disabilities
esa_eligible_grades: Students with qualifying disabilities; private school, home school and co-enrollment options
esa_uses_other_platform: NCSEAA portal / ESA+ DirectPay/provider payment processes
esa_application_deadline: Annual application cycle; 2025-26 opened February 1, 2026 per NCSEAA page
esa_notes: Prompt's NC Opportunity Scholarship is a voucher, but NC's ESA-style program is ESA+ for children with disabilities.

name: North Dakota
program_status: not active from verified sources; 2025 ESA bill passed legislature but veto/status conflict flagged
esa_notes: Search results conflict: advocacy sources celebrated passage, bill tracking shows HB1540 vetoed/Filed with Secretary of State. No official parent portal found. Treat as not active until North Dakota DPI posts implementation.

name: Nebraska
program_status: no active state ESA found; federal scholarship tax-credit opt-in moving for 2027
esa_portal_name: Opportunity Scholarships of Nebraska / federal tax credit opt-in information
esa_admin: No active state ESA admin identified
esa_notes: Prompt listed LB295/Learning Acceleration/EFA, but this pass did not verify an active state ESA portal. Nebraska's earlier scholarship/tax-credit fights have changed repeatedly.

name: New Hampshire
esa_admin: Children's Scholarship Fund New Hampshire; NH Department of Education oversight
esa_funding_amount: Average account value reported around $4,795 for 2025-26; amount varies
esa_eligible_grades: NH resident students ages 5-20 entering K-12; universal with priority/cap rules
esa_application_deadline: Applications for 2026-27 open; submit one application per family
esa_notes: Priority access for current EFA recipients, siblings, special-needs students, and families at/below 350% FPL; others may waitlist if demand exceeds cap.

name: Ohio
esa_admin: Ohio Department of Education and Workforce
esa_funding_amount: $6,166 K-8; $8,408 grades 9-12 maximum voucher amount
esa_eligible_grades: K-12 private-school scholarship/voucher eligibility; income sliding scale
esa_uses_other_platform: ODEW scholarship application through participating private schools
esa_application_deadline: 2025-26 application open through June 30, 2026; verify 2026-27 window
esa_max_reimbursement: Voucher amount only; not receipt reimbursement ESA
esa_notes: Included because prompt listed it, but it is a voucher/scholarship, not an ESA wallet.

name: Oklahoma
program_status: active refundable tax credit, not ESA
esa_admin: Oklahoma Tax Commission
esa_funding_amount: Private school refundable credit $5,000-$7,500; homeschool tax credit up to $1,000/student
esa_eligible_grades: Eligible Oklahoma taxpayers/students; homeschool credit claimed on tax return, no application portal
esa_application_deadline: 2026-27 private school application closed; homeschool credit claimed with tax return via Form 591-D
esa_reimbursement_url: Tax return / Form 591-D for homeschool expenses
esa_notes: No ESA wallet. Homeschool families keep receipts and claim tax credit, which creates materially different workflow/compliance requirements.

name: South Carolina
esa_admin: South Carolina Department of Education; ClassWallet contracted for support/distribution
esa_eligible_grades: Qualifying K-12 South Carolina families; income-based eligibility/caps
esa_application_deadline: 2026-27 application closed; statutory limit of 15,000 students reached
esa_notes: ClassWallet digital wallet; watch caps/waitlist.

name: Tennessee
program_status: active; EFS statewide plus ESA pilot/IEA programs
esa_portal_name: Education Freedom Scholarship / Education Savings Account Program
esa_admin: Tennessee Department of Education; Student First Technologies/Theodore portal for EFS
esa_funding_amount: EFS 2025-26 amount cited $7,295; ESA pilot 2026-27 estimate $10,148.88 in ESA zones; final amounts may change
esa_eligible_grades: K-12 Tennessee students under EFS; ESA pilot specific zones include Memphis-Shelby, Metro Nashville, Hamilton; IEA special education program exists separately
esa_application_deadline: EFS 2026-27 application closed February 6, 2026; ESA school/provider reopening May 29-June 12, 2026 noted for schools/providers
esa_reimbursement_url: EFS/TDOE portal; ESA pilot processes through TDOE
esa_notes: Do not collapse Tennessee into one program. NCSL lists EFS, ESA Pilot, and IEA. The prompt's '3 counties expanding' maps to ESA pilot zones, while EFS is broader/newer.

name: Texas
esa_admin: Texas Comptroller of Public Accounts; Odyssey portal support
esa_funding_amount: 2026-27: $10,474 private school; up to $30,000 IEP private school; $2,000 homeschool/other
esa_eligible_grades: Texas children outside public system including private school or homeschool; priority tiers by disability/income
esa_application_deadline: Parent application window ran February 4-March 31, 2026
esa_max_reimbursement: $2,000 homeschool; $10,474 private; up to $30,000 disability private placement
esa_notes: Official FAQ lists common ineligibility/rejection reasons: pre-K criteria, income verification, citizenship/lawful presence, Texas residency. Massive application volume and waitlist should be assumed.

name: Utah
esa_admin: Program manager: ACE Scholarships / Utah Fits All; Utah State Board of Education oversight reference
esa_funding_amount: Private school $8,000; home-based ages 5-11 $4,000; home-based ages 12-18 $6,000
esa_eligible_grades: All Utah resident K-12 students; funding cap/waitlist
esa_application_deadline: 2026-27 applications closed
esa_max_reimbursement: Award amount; prorated for mid-year entry; rollover rules apply
esa_notes: Amounts differ for home-based vs private-school students starting 2025-26. Odyssey appears in current support/marketplace materials.

name: Wisconsin
esa_portal_name: Wisconsin Parental Choice Programs / Special Needs Scholarship Program
esa_admin: Wisconsin Department of Public Instruction
esa_funding_amount: WPCP 2025-26 voucher $10,877 K-8 / $13,371 9-12; SNSP estimated $16,049
esa_eligible_grades: Private-school choice program applicants; SNSP for qualifying disabled students
esa_application_deadline: 2026-27 application periods vary by program/month
esa_notes: Included because prompt listed it. Wisconsin choice/SNSP programs are vouchers/scholarships, not ESA wallets.

name: West Virginia
esa_admin: West Virginia Hope Scholarship Board / State Treasurer
esa_funding_amount: Expected $5,435.62 for 2026-27 reported by West Virginia Watch; program amount tied to state funding formula
esa_eligible_grades: K-12 West Virginia students; universal eligibility starts 2026-27
esa_application_deadline: Full-funding deadline for new 2026-27 students: June 15, 2026
esa_notes: Litigation history exists, but program is operating. Academic reporting and accepted tests are a recurring parent confusion point.

name: Wyoming
program_status: active; Steamboat Legacy expansion moving forward for 2026-27
esa_portal_name: Steamboat Legacy Scholarship / Wyoming Education Savings Account
esa_admin: Wyoming Department of Education; Odyssey support materials
esa_funding_amount: $7,000 annually per eligible student
esa_eligible_grades: Universal eligibility beginning 2025-26/2026-27 implementation; pre-K eligibility has income limits under expansion notes
esa_application_deadline: 2026-27 timelines being finalized in May 2026 WDE update; verify current portal
esa_notes: Renamed/expanded by Steamboat Legacy Scholarship Act. WDE GovDelivery noted 2026-27 program moving forward, but application timeline still being finalized.
